response.reasoning: Candidate has 15+ years of software development experience, far exceeding the 6+ year requirement. Current role at re:cinq (April 2025-present) directly involves full-stack TypeScript/Node.js work plus AI-agent infrastructure and MCP code generators, which maps precisely to the 'AI agent development' requirement. Prior Optilyz role used TypeScript, React, MongoDB, AWS — covering frontend, backend, NoSQL, and cloud. RIDE Capital experience shows TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development with Node/TS/GraphQL. Dunnhumby experience demonstrates high-scale microservices. Skills list confirms TypeScript, MongoDB, Docker, SQL, Java, Git. Gaps: no explicit mention of Nest.js, Vitest, Kotlin, Postgres, or Kubernetes in resume text (Kubernetes mentioned for GISCollective deployment). Polyglot background (TS, Java, D, C++, Python implied) fits 'multiple programming languages' requirement. Strong teaching/community track record indicates communication skills. BS in Computer Science meets education requirement. Location is Lagos, Nigeria but most prior roles were Berlin-based — may or may not matter depending on remote policy.
strengths: 15+ years full-stack experience with TypeScript, Node.js, React, MongoDB, AWS
Direct AI-agent and MCP framework experience at current role
Strong engineering discipline: TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, testing focus
concerns: No explicit Nest.js or Vitest experience listed
Kotlin/PostgreSQL not mentioned; Java experience is older
Location (Lagos) vs. likely Berlin/EU team — timezone/remote fit unclear
response.justification: Candidate substantially exceeds the experience bar and hits the core stack (TypeScript, Node, React, MongoDB, AWS, Docker) plus the AI-agent requirement directly through current MCP/agentic work. Missing explicit Nest.js and Vitest is a minor concern for a senior with this breadth — both are quickly picked up given his Node.js and testing-framework background. Strong testing culture, polyglot range, and architecture exposure make him a clear interview.
